errors.message: The field at path '/searchDashClustersByAll/metadata/entityResultAttributes/templateType' was declared as a non null type, but the code involved in retrieving data has wrongly returned a null value.  The graphql specification requires that the parent field be set to null, or if that is non nullable that it bubble up null to its parent and so on. The non-nullable type is 'dash_search_EntityResultTemplate' within parent type 'dash_search_EntityResultAttributes'

summary.text:
#Hiring: Senior Python ETL Developer (AWS Glue)- Only W2
Location: Remote
Contract: 6–12+ Months 
Experience: 6+ Years (Genuine)

Note:
Strong hands-on experience with AWS Glue & Python ETL is mandatory

Role Overview:
We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Python ETL Developer to design and build scalable data transformation solutions with a strong focus on RBQM (Risk-Based Quality Management). The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in AWS Glue, modern data processing frameworks, and building robust ETL pipelines.

Must-Have Skills:
✔ 8+ years of Python development with strong ETL experience
✔ 6+ years working with Polars, Pandas, PySpark for data transformation
✔ 6+ years of AWS Cloud experience (AWS Glue mandatory)
✔ Strong expertise in building scalable ETL pipelines & transformation layers
✔ Experience with modern data processing frameworks
✔ 5+ years with:
• Unit testing (Pytest / unittest)
• Mocking frameworks (Moto)
• CI/CD pipelines using GitHub workflows
✔ Strong understanding of:
• Data engineering best practices
• Performance tuning & scalable architecture
• AI-assisted coding tools/agents

Key Responsibilities:
• Design & develop Python-based ETL frameworks and reusable packages
• Build and optimize data pipelines using AWS Glue & PySpark
• Perform large-scale data transformations using Polars & Pandas
• Ensure high code quality through testing frameworks
• Develop CI/CD pipelines and automate deployments
• Implement testing strategies using AWS mocking tools (Moto)
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver scalable solutions
• Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize ETL workflows

📩 Interested candidates can share their resume at:
ravi2@gretisglobal.com

Key Responsibilities

summary.text:
We are hiring for a Senior Data Scientist with 5–7 years of experience to join our team in Toronto on a contract basis. This role is ideal for a data storyteller who excels at the intersection of complex mathematics and business strategy, turning massive datasets into a competitive advantage.

Key Responsibilities:
- Lead the design, development, and deployment of production-grade machine learning models.
- Conduct strategic exploratory data analysis (EDA) to uncover patterns in structured and unstructured data.
- Collaborate with Data Engineers to build robust, automated workflows and scalable data architectures.
- Translate complex technical findings into elegant dashboards using Power BI or Tableau for stakeholder understanding.
- Stay ahead of the curve by experimenting with NLP, Deep Learning, and the latest MLOps practices.

Qualifications:
- 5–7 years in a dedicated Data Science role with a proven track record of deploying models to production.
- Mastery of Python and SQL, with deep experience in Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.
- Strong foundation in statistics, probability, and predictive modeling.
- Experience working within AWS, Azure, or GCP, and handling large-scale data using Spark or Hadoop.
- Bonus points for experience with Docker/Kubernetes or specialized knowledge in NLP or Computer Vision.

If you are ready to make an impact through data-driven discovery, we would love to hear from you.

#DataScience #MachineLearning #Hiring #AI #BigData #PythonJobs

🚀 Hiring: AI Engineer (Snowflake + RAG)
📍 Location: Work From Anywhere
📄 Job Type: Contract (Full-Time)
⏳ Duration: 3 Months
💼 Experience: 5 – 8 Years
💡 Role Overview
We are looking for an AI Engineer with strong experience in Snowflake Data Warehouse and AI-driven analytics. The role involves building scalable data pipelines and contributing to AI-powered applications like conversational analytics using Snowflake Cortex LLM.
🛠️ Mandatory Skills
✔ RAG Frameworks (5–8 Years)
✔ Snowflake (5–8 Years)
⭐ Good to Have
✔ Power BI (3–5 Years)
✔ Tableau (3–5 Years)
✔ Python (3–5 Years)
🔧 Key Responsibilities
📊 1. Snowflake Data Engineering
Develop data models (Star / Snowflake schema)
Build & optimize ELT pipelines
Write efficient SQL queries & transformations
Handle structured & semi-structured data ingestion
Optimize warehouse performance & cost
Implement RBAC & governance
🤖 2. AI & Cortex LLM Support
Build AI agents using Snowflake Cortex LLM
Work on RAG workflows & vector embeddings
Assist in prompt engineering & testing
Validate AI outputs for accuracy & security
💬 3. Conversational Analytics
Develop natural language query-based analytics tools
Integrate with Power BI / Tableau
Translate business KPIs into dashboards
Ensure data accuracy & performance
🤝 4. Collaboration & Delivery
Work with architects & senior stakeholders
Participate in Agile/sprint planning
Maintain documentation
Provide production support
🎯 Required Qualifications
✔ 5–7 years in Data / Analytics Engineering
✔ 3–4 years hands-on experience in Snowflake
✔ Strong SQL expertise (joins, window functions, tuning)
✔ Working knowledge of Python
✔ Strong data modeling concepts
➕ Preferred Skills
✔ Exposure to RAG / semantic search
✔ Knowledge of data governance & security
✔ Experience with JSON, Parquet
✔ Domain exposure (HR / Sales / Finance / Supply Chain)
